root_text.ud4.10:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud4.10:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā sāriputto bhagavato avidūre nisinno hoti pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya attano upasamaṁ paccavekkhamāno.
root_text.ud4.10:2.1: Addasā kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ avidūre nisinnaṁ pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya attano upasamaṁ paccavekkhamānaṁ.
root_text.ud4.10:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud4.10:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud4.10:1.3: Now at that time Venerable Sāriputta was sitting not far from the Buddha, cross-legged, with his body straight, reviewing his own peacefulness.
translation_text.ud4.10:2.1: The Buddha saw him meditating there.
translation_text.ud4.10:3.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud4.10:4.1: “With mind at peace, so full of peace,
translation_text.ud4.10:4.2: for a mendicant who has cut the cord,

variant_text.ud4.10:5.1: Meghiyo uddhatā gopālo → meghiyaṁ uddhataṁ gopālaṁ (bj)
variant_text.ud4.10:5.2: yakkho → juṇhaṁ (bj, mr); juṇhā (si, sya-all, pts-vp-pli1)
